mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate short-term discount rate pressure, forcing institutional portfolios into equities and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 9.5% signal severe labor market distress that suppresses consumer spending expectations, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic recovery, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with >55% accuracy, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines below 98.0 indicate manufacturing contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of observations when INDPRO < 98.0, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with persistently high unemployment create a liquidity trap environment where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, leading to negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign or becomes insignificant, and 20-day returns remain positive despite the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600,000 units signal a stalled residential construction sector that drags down related supply chains and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered periods, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect improving factory utilization and output, which generates short-term momentum in cyclical equity sectors and positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias or are negative in >50% of observations following INDPRO increases, with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines exceeding 200,000 in a single month indicate accelerating job losses that force earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months with PAYEMS declines > 200k, or the signal fails to maintain predictive power in walk-forward tests.
